label: IND Application Reporting - Protocol Amendments
title: IND Application Reporting: Protocol Amendments | FDA
text: Sponsors are expected to submit protocol amendments for new protocols or changes to existing protocols before implementation. Changes that significantly affect subject safety, scope, or scientific quality require an amendment. FDA examples include increasing dosage or exposure beyond the current protocol, significant design changes such as adding or removing a control group, and adding or removing safety-monitoring tests. A change intended to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard may be implemented immediately, provided FDA is subsequently notified by protocol amendment and the reviewing IRB is also notified.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Once an IND application is in effect, the sponsor may amend the application as needed to ensure that clinical investigations are conducted according to protocols included in the IND application.

sections.heading: Timing
sections.text: Sponsors are expected to submit protocol amendments for new protocols or changes to existing protocols before implementation of the respective changes.

sections.heading: Types
sections.text: Examples include dose or exposure increases, significant protocol design changes, and changes to safety-monitoring tests. Immediate-hazard changes may be implemented immediately with subsequent FDA and IRB notification.

citation_spans.quoted_text: Sponsors are expected to submit protocol amendments for new protocols or changes to existing protocols before implementation of the respective changes.
Dose increases, significant design changes, and safety-monitoring changes may require amendment; immediate-hazard changes may be implemented with subsequent FDA and IRB notification.
